items.headline: Russia Oil Revenues Halved in March Before War Boost
items.summary: Russian producers paid 494.9 billion rubles ($6.18 billion) in oil taxes last month, down 48 percent year on year, according to Bloomberg calculations based on Finance Ministry data released Friday.

items.headline: Russia’s Oil Revenues Halved in March Before Iran War Boost
items.summary: Russia’s oil tax revenue in March dropped by nearly half compared to a year earlier, highlighting the Kremlin’s financial strain just before the Middle East war delivered an unexpected boost to Moscow’s earnings.

items.headline: Pakistan's Military Chief Asim Munir Emerges as Trump's Key Interlocutor in Iran Crisis
items.summary: Field Marshal Asim Munir secured direct access to Trump administration and played decisive role in Iran-US ceasefire talks. Pakistan's dual-track diplomacy elevated it to strategic standing comparable to Israel in Middle East crisis.

dimension_signal.rationale: Successfully mediated between US and Iran, demonstrating responsible leadership in preventing escalation

items.headline: IAEA Reports Complete Loss of Access to Iran's Nuclear Facilities
items.summary: International Atomic Energy Agency confirms no access to any of Iran's four declared enrichment facilities since February 2026. Agency cannot verify current status of enriched uranium stockpiles or Isfahan Fuel Enrichment Plant.

items.headline: Iran's Intelligence Chief Majid Khademi Killed in Israeli Strike
items.summary: Major General Majid Khademi, intelligence chief of Iran's Revolutionary Guard, confirmed killed by Israeli forces. Israel's Defense Minister threatens to continue hunting Iranian leaders one by one.

items.headline: US-Israeli Strikes Hit Sharif University of Technology in Tehran
items.summary: Attacks targeted Iran's leading scientific university, compared to MIT, with extensive damage to laboratories and mosque. Iranian officials say strikes aim to wipe out Iranian identity and education infrastructure.

items.headline: Iran Forms Human Chains Around Power Plants Ahead of Trump's Deadline
items.summary: Iranian state media shows civilians forming human shields at Kazerun power plant hours before Trump's 8 PM ET deadline. Iran rejected US temporary ceasefire proposal, countered with permanent end to war demands.

dimension_signal.rationale: Set arbitrary 8 PM deadline threatening to destroy Iran's civilization, creating unnecessary time pressure

items.headline: Two US Fighter Jets Shot Down by Iranian Forces on Friday
items.summary: F-15 Strike Eagle crashed in Iran with one crew member missing, second combat plane downed near Strait of Hormuz. Search and rescue operations underway as war enters sixth week with 2,076 Iranian civilians killed.

items.headline: Modi Coalition Under Pressure as Pakistan's Mediation Success Marginalizes India
items.summary: India relegated to single phone call from Trump while Pakistan gains diplomatic capital as key Middle East mediator. External Affairs Minister Jaishankar called Pakistan a 'dalal' (fixer), betraying profound marginalization.

dimension_signal.rationale: Images of undocumented Indians deported from US shattered Modi's claim of special friendship with Trump

items.headline: Netanyahu Coalition Advances Bill to Eliminate Fraud Charges Against PM

dimension_signal.rationale: Advancing legislation to retroactively eliminate criminal charges he faces shows avoidance of legal accountability

items.headline: IAEA Director General: Iranian Nuclear Program Cannot Be Eliminated by Military Action Alone
items.summary: Rafael Grossi states Iran's nuclear program is 'very vast' and 'scattered across a number of places' including facilities, universities and laboratories. IAEA doesn't know status of new Isfahan enrichment facility.

items.headline: Iranian Foreign Minister Credits Pakistan's 'Tireless Efforts' in Ceasefire Deal
items.summary: Abbas Araghchi expressed gratitude to PM Sharif and Field Marshal Munir for mediation efforts. Iran agreed to halt operations and reopen Strait of Hormuz for two weeks based on Pakistan's brotherly request.
